content:
Hello, I am really new to software dev and just wanted to ask how to build the mental model required to contribute properly. I have actually sent small safety patches back in april and was looking at other opportunities to contribute but even easy first issues marked as medium kind of seemed difficult to me.......

I have actually started reading Philipp Oppermann's writing an OS in rust, but what I wanted to ask is like, I am able to understand what a specific module is trying to do (atleast the easy ones ig..) but how do you build the mental model to make changes in it?

Sorry if this conversation is out of place for this chat but I'd really appreciate it if someone can tell me how they started with contributing properly to the kernel code.

Thanks.
This topic was moved here from #General > Seeking Guidance by Miguel Ojeda.

I took a quick look at what you sent, and they seem generally fine to me, i.e. do not worry too much, you seem to know how to contribute already :)

The patches may not have been picked up yet (sync and workqueue, right?), but I assume they will eventually be. I could perhaps pick them if needed too. Cc @Boqun Feng @Gary Guo @Alice Ryhl

Regarding the rest, I would suggest tackling an area you may like, and perhaps reviewing patches from others in the mailing list -- it is a good way to learn and to discover areas you may like to contribute to.

I hope that helps!

I think these are the patches you are waiting on?

atomic
https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260428033935.16875-1-sagartaunk2@gmail.com/

workqueue
https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260426175317.10171-1-sagartaunk2@gmail.com/
https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260430071930.73696-1-sagartaunk2@gmail.com/
https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260430115428.48087-1-sagartaunk2@gmail.com/
If I missed something, please add them here!

Yes, these are the ones. Thanks, but what I wanted to ask was like, I am able to understand what the modules are doing (for the most part) but not like why are they used in certain places and stuff....
Though, I think if i keep trying to read other's patches I probably will eventually be able to. I was mostly just confused about how one navigates and connects such large codebases....
Thanks again, I will keep reading and trying to find some easy patches to work on for now.
Sagar Taunk has marked this topic as resolved.
